repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.physdesc: Correspondence, meeting minutes, journals, field notebooks, photographs.
did.abstract: Journals and field notes relating to Mexican trips, 1970s; correspondence relating to professional matters, including the Cornell Plantations; journals, 1950s-1970s; and miscellaneous field notes, herbarium records, and teaching records.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

defitem.label: EAD encoding:
defitem.item: Peter Martinez and Carolyn Smith, November, 2003

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Journals and field notes relating to Mexican trips, 1970s; correspondence relating to professional matters, including the Cornell Plantations; journals, 1950s-1970s; and miscellaneous field notes, herbarium records, and teaching records.

emph.ref: Series I. Notebooks from Research Trips/Journals
Series III. Cornell Plantations - Correspondence and Meeting Minutes
Series V. Data on Finger Lakes Region Plants

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Robert T. Clausen papers, #34-1-2585. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

did.unittitle: Series I. Notebooks from Research Trips/Journals
Research trips to Mexico and the Western U.S.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Forming the basis of Clausen's study on Sedum of the Mexican Plateau. In addition to discussing the specimens, geololgy, and vegetation of the areas canvasssed, Clausen recorded details on the logistics of the trips

did.unittitle: Field records from his research trips

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Field records from his research trips, including Glaciated Allegheny Plateau, 1975-80; Sedimentary Appalachian Mts., 1966; Central Rocky Mts., 1966, Red Mtn., Mammoth, Devils River, 1970; Cascade Mts.; Piedmont Plateau: San Bernadino Mts., 1940, 1963, 1968. Additionally, Clausen kept records of his interests in Ithaca and Cornell, such as the Herbarium; Greenhouse in Slaterville Springs; Botany Courses, 1964-5; Taxonomy Courses, 1966; and his personal possesions at Cornell, 1971.

did.unittitle: Journals - Clausen recorded items from both his personal and professional life.
Journals, trip notebooks, field notes, agenda, experiment records
